title: Contribution #4 — tutorial with a biological claim (Domain-reviewer path)
context: Some tutorials for cancer research tools necessarily include a biological or clinical claim — for example, explaining what a mutational signature implies about cancer etiology, or what a differential-expression result means biologically. Such claims require domain-expert verification (bioinformatician; oncologist if clinical context) to ensure they are accurate and do not constitute medical advice. This is the first task in the project to exercise the Domain-reviewer path, elevating riskTier to medium. Depends on pilot-007, triage-009, and repro-004.
objective: Author and contribute a merged tutorial for a gate-passed tool that includes at least one biological/clinical claim, following the Domain-reviewer path: every assertion cited, Domain reviewer sign-off obtained, 'not medical advice' disclaimer present, and tutorial runs reproducibly in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: Target tool has a PASS gate artifact from triage-009 confirming OSI-permissive license and cancer-data guardrail clearance; example data is shipped/synthetic or verified open-access.
Issue-first alignment confirmed with the maintainer before authoring.
Every biological or clinical assertion in the tutorial carries an inline citation to a primary or authoritative source; no assertion ships without provenance.
riskTier is medium; the mandatory 'research/education use — not medical advice' disclaimer is present and prominent.
Domain reviewer (credentialed bioinformatician; oncologist where clinical context appears) sign-off obtained confirming accuracy and absence of medical advice.
License + Data-provenance reviewer sign-off and Technical reviewer sign-off obtained.
Tutorial runs reproducibly in CI from the pinned environment (reproEnv.ciRunRef recorded).
Contribution merged upstream; outcome artifact committed at outcomes/onco-research-software-docs-contrib-013.json with merge-commit URL and coverage before/after.
output: A tutorial with biological claims merged upstream for a gate-passed tool, with Domain reviewer sign-off, 'not medical advice' disclaimer, reproducible CI run, and outcome artifact recorded — exercising the full Domain-reviewer path for the first time.
